ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange web-shell drop via w3wp.exe under OWA/ECP/aspnet_client
ucs.description: Hunts ASPX/ASHX web shells being written by the IIS worker process or Exchange UM worker into the OWA, ECP, or aspnet_client virtual directories — the exact post-ProxyLogon persistence pattern Microsoft attributed to HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon (Operation Exchange Marauder, CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065). Catches both the original 2021 mass-exploitation pattern and HAFNIUM's continued Exchange Server abuse.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.action IN ("created","modified","renamed") AND (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.asp") AND (Filesystem.file_path="*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\ClientAccess\\Owa\\auth\\*" OR Filesystem.file_path="*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*") AND (Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" OR Filesystem.process_name="UMWorkerProcess.exe") by host Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_name Filesystem.file_path Filesystem.file_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename file_hash as file_sha256 | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | where NOT match(file_name, "(?i)^(owaauth|logon|signout|errorfe|exporttool)\.aspx$")
ucs.kql:
// HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon — web-shell deployment via IIS worker into Exchange virtual dirs
let lookback = 7d;
let _hafnium_known_names = dynamic(["web.aspx","help.aspx","document.aspx","errorEE.aspx","errorEEE.aspx","errorEW.aspx","errorFF.aspx","healthcheck.aspx","aspnet_www.aspx","aspnet_client.aspx","xx.aspx","shell.aspx","aspnet_iisstart.aspx","one.aspx","supp0rt.aspx","HttpProxy.aspx"]);
DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx" or FileName endswith ".asp"
| where FolderPath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\", @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe")
| extend KnownHafniumName = iff(tolower(FileName) in (_hafnium_known_names), true, false)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName,
          InitiatingProcessAccountName, InitiatingProcessAccountDomain,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, KnownHafniumName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon — web-shell drop seen by ASIM file telemetry
let lookback = 7d;
ImFileEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventType in ("FileCreated","FileModified","FileRenamed")
| where TargetFileName endswith ".aspx" or TargetFileName endswith ".ashx" or TargetFileName endswith ".asp"
| where TargetFilePath has_any (@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\", @"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\", @"\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\", @"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\")
| where ActingProcessName has_any ("w3wp.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe","umworkerprocess.exe")
| project TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          ActingProcessName, ActingProcessCommandLine,
          TargetFileName, TargetFilePath, TargetFileSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon Exchange Web Shell Drop via IIS Worker
id: 7c5f3a8c-1f7e-4f86-9d2e-3b0c2b6f2a91
status: experimental
description: ASPX/ASHX web shell written by w3wp.exe or UMWorkerProcess.exe under Exchange OWA/ECP/aspnet_client virtual directories — the canonical HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon post-ProxyLogon persistence pattern.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/11
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe'
      - '\UMWorkerProcess.exe'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\'
      - '\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\'
      - '\ClientAccess\Owa\auth\'
      - '\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\'
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.aspx'
      - '.ashx'
      - '.asp'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Exchange cumulative update installs that legitimately write aspx files under FrontEnd\HttpProxy paths (verify via correlation with KBxxx install)
level: high
tags:
  - attack.initial_access
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1505.003
  - attack.g0125
ucs.rationale: This isn't a generic web-shell rule — it specifically scopes to (a) Exchange's IIS worker (w3wp) and UM worker process as the writer, and (b) the precise filesystem locations Microsoft, Volexity, and CISA called out as HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon ProxyLogon drop sites. The actor's signature is the unauthenticated SSRF (CVE-2021-26855) into Exchange combined with arbitrary file-write that puts script cont

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) LSASS credential dump via Procdump on Exchange-class hosts
ucs.description: Hunts Sysinternals procdump (or renamed) invoked against lsass.exe — the credential-access step HAFNIUM operators performed immediately after web-shell establishment on compromised Exchange Servers, prior to staging the dump with 7-Zip and exfil via the shell. Matches the actor's documented OPSEC: living-off-the-land using a signed, allow-listed Microsoft tool.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="procdump.exe" OR Processes.process_name="procdump64.exe" OR Processes.original_file_name="procdump" OR Processes.original_file_name="procdump64") AND (Processes.process="*lsass*" OR Processes.process="*-ma *" OR Processes.process="*-mp *") by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.original_file_name Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user, "(?i)\$$") | convert ctime(firstTime) ctime(lastTime) | sort - lastTime
ucs.kql:
// HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon — procdump (or renamed) dumping lsass post-Exchange-compromise
let lookback = 7d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe")
      or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ ("procdump","procdump64")
      or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has "Sysinternals procdump")
| where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass", " -ma ", " -mp ", "-ma lsass")
| extend RenamedBinary = iff(
      not(FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe"))
          and ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName has "procdump",
      true, false)
| extend OnExchange = iff(InitiatingProcessParentFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe")
                       or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","umworkerprocess.exe","cmd.exe","powershell.exe"), true, false)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, AccountDomain,
          FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine,
          OriginalFileName = ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName,
          SHA256, RenamedBinary, OnExchange,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessParentFileName
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon — procdump-vs-lsass on Sentinel-side process telemetry
let lookback = 7d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where (NewProcessName endswith "\\procdump.exe"
      or NewProcessName endswith "\\procdump64.exe"
      or CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)procdump(64)?\b")
| where CommandLine has_any ("lsass"," -ma "," -mp ")
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, SubjectUserName, IpAddress
| union (
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where (TargetProcessName endswith "\\procdump.exe"
          or TargetProcessName endswith "\\procdump64.exe"
          or ActingProcessFileOriginalName has "procdump")
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass"," -ma "," -mp ")
    | project TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname,
              Account = ActorUsername,
              NewProcessName = TargetProcessName,
              CommandLine = TargetProcessCommandLine,
              ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType = ActingProcessIntegrityLevel,
              SubjectUserName = ActorUsername, IpAddress = DvcIpAddr
)
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon LSASS Dump via Procdump
id: b8a1c6f4-3e4b-4f1d-8b22-2f8a5d9c4e02
status: experimental
description: Procdump (Sysinternals) — original or renamed — invoked against lsass.exe to collect credentials. Matches the credential-access step performed by HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon following ProxyLogon-based Exchange compromise.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2021/03/02/hafnium-targeting-exchange-servers/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0125/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa21-062a
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/11
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_binary:
    - Image|endswith:
        - '\procdump.exe'
        - '\procdump64.exe'
    - OriginalFileName:
        - 'procdump'
        - 'procdump64'
  selection_target:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'lsass'
      - ' -ma '
      - ' -mp '
  condition: selection_binary and selection_target
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IR / forensic collection by SOC staff (allow-list by user/host)
  - Microsoft-supported lsass memory captures for support cases
level: high
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.g0125
ucs.rationale: Microsoft's HAFNIUM write-up and the CISA AA21-062A advisory both name procdump-against-lsass as Silk Typhoon's go-to credential-access primitive after web-shell establishment; Volexity confirmed the same chain (`procdump -ma lsass.exe`) on Exchange Server victims. The rule fires on (a) canonical procdump binary names, (b) the SysInternals-signed OriginalFileName even when the operator renames the
